column1: # Section 232 auto parts — per-prefix applicability shares
# Note 33(g) enumerates the HTSUS provisions in which "parts of passenger
# vehicles ... and light trucks" may be classified
# headings 9903.94.05/.06 is being such a part — not mere classifiability.
# Most enumerated provisions are vehicle-specific (e.g. 4009.12.0020 hoses "of
# a kind used in motor vehicles")
column2: so binary inclusion is fine. A few broad

column1: # prefixes sweep in large dual-use trade that is not vehicle parts.
# applicability_share = fraction of the prefix's imports that are in-scope
# vehicle parts. share = 0 excludes the prefix from the heading's EFFECTIVE
# keeps the literal-enumeration rate (heading rate minus auto rebate) via
# step 7d of 06_calculate_rates.R - so the statutory-vs-collected wedge on
# these lines stays measurable in tariff-etr-eval rather than baked into the
# model. 0 < share < 1 scales the heading rate (blending
# qualifying_share; note fractional scaling currently feeds statutory too).
# Prefixes not listed default to 1.0. Longest prefix wins.

column3: Bare heading 8471 is on the Note 33(g) list, but general-purpose ADP machines (laptops, servers, storage units) are complete machines, not parts of passenger vehicles; vehicle ECUs classify under 8537/9032/8708. Census IMDB collections on Taiwan 8471 ($139B trade, ~0.8% collected vs 23.8% tracked) imply ~zero de facto auto-parts application. Interim binary calibration 2026-06-04 (extreme-eta review item 1); refine if CBP entry data shows vehicle-use 8471 trade. Note 39 semi lines (8471.50/.80 on s232_semi_products.csv) are unaffected - they are stripped from auto lists before this filter and keep their semi rate.
